title: Euphrasia litoralis
intro: Euphrasia litoralis, also known as seaside eyebright, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the genus Euphrasia, commonly used in traditional herbal medicine. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe and calm the eyes, as well as alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Euphrasia litoralis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Euphrasia litoralis include eye drops, capsules, and tinctures, often used to treat eye infections, conjunctivitis, and other eye-related conditions.
taxonomy: Euphrasia litoralis is classified as a species within the family Orobanchaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as broomrapes or Indian paintbrushes. Euphrasia litoralis is also classified as a member of the genus Euphrasia, which comprises several species of flowering plants. The genus Euphrasia is a group of hemiparasitic plants found in various parts of the world, including Europe, Asia, and North Africa. These plants are known for their small, delicate flowers and their parasitic relationship with other plants.
uses: Euphrasia litoralis, also known as sea eyebright, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Its leaves and flowers can be used to make infusions or tinctures, which are believed to have a soothing effect on the eyes and skin. Some studies suggest that Euphrasia litoralis may have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, making it a potential treatment for minor wounds and infections. The plant's extracts have been used to treat conjunctivitis, conjunctival inflammation, and other eye problems. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Euphrasia litoralis for medicinal use.

parts: Euphrasia litoralis is a species of Euphrasia commonly known as coastal eyebright. The medicinal parts of Euphrasia litoralis are primarily its leaves, stems, and flowers. preparations: Euphrasia litoralis preparations are made from its leaves and flowers, which are used in herbal medicine due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
